meta.title: Eva-CLI | Multi-Agent Runtime
meta.description: Eva-CLI is an open-source V1.11.5-alpha CLI runtime with V1.17.6 V1.x closure release gate evidence for controlled multi-Agent workflows, combining Rust runtime boundaries, Lua capability layers, Topic EventBus routing, memory and knowledge context, adapters, hardware plans, release gates, durable evidence, observability policy, operator safety fields, public JSON contract validation, closure reporting, and runtime command completion.

home.heroTagline: A controlled runtime for multi-Agent automation
home.heroLead: Eva-CLI uses Rust for reliable runtime boundaries and Lua for hot-reloadable Agent behavior, then connects external Agents, MCP servers, skills, memory, knowledge bases, and hardware through a unified Topic EventBus.

runtimeSteps.label: Rust Core
runtimeSteps.description: Owns policy, memory, recovery, and process boundaries.

runtimeSteps.label: Lua Agents
runtimeSteps.description: Hot-reloads behavior while keeping capability access explicit.

runtimeSteps.label: Adapters
runtimeSteps.description: Routes CLI, MCP, HTTP, skills, and hardware through permissions.

home.runtimePanelNote: V1.17.6 keeps the V1.11.5-alpha runtime version and adds the V1.x closure release gate over the wider evidence line: daemon control, provider supervision gates, restore mutation/rollback evidence, service-manager abstraction, hardware safety, memory and observability policy, operator execution fields, high-risk text summaries, public JSON contract validation, and external blocker reporting.
home.progressTitle: From architecture scaffold to release evidence, operator safety, and closure gates
home.progressCurrentTitle: V1.17.6 V1.x closure release gate is in place
home.progressCurrentBody: The repository keeps the V1.11.5-alpha source version while documenting and gating the completed V1.12 to V1.17 runtime evidence line: daemon control and recovery, provider supervision gates, restore mutation and rollback evidence, service-manager abstraction, hardware safety gates, memory retrieval and redaction, observability retention policy, run command split, operator execution fields, high-risk text summaries, public JSON contract diff validation, and the V1.x closure report.
progressNextItems: Resolve production signing, attestation, OS package publication, and release upload blockers when credentials and repository permissions exist.
Attach platform service-manager test environments, real hardware fixtures, and production database sink evidence before claiming production readiness.
Keep high-risk restore, upgrade, hardware, and lifecycle paths behind explicit evidence, confirmation gates, and stable JSON contracts.
home.featuredBlogTitle: Latest design notes from the blog
home.featuredBlogBody: Follow the implementation thinking behind Eva-CLI: runtime boundaries, LuaAgent tooling, product direction, and controlled automation design.

progressStages.label: Architecture, module partitioning, and configuration
progressStages.description: System boundaries, runtime model, adapters, memory, discovery, hardware, recovery, website, docs, configuration, and module partitioning are in place.

progressStages.label: Runnable V1.0 runtime and task diagnostics
progressStages.description: The in-memory basic loop, CLI quickstart, local task status/log/cancel diagnostics, and core CI checks are implemented.

progressStages.label: V1.1 to V1.5.1 controlled capability, release gates, and GHCR delivery
progressStages.description: External capability diagnostics, memory context, hardware plans, backup/lifecycle checks, release hardening commands, the source user manual, and GitHub Packages container delivery are implemented.

progressStages.label: V1.17.6 V1.x closure release gate
progressStages.description: The V1.12 daemon, V1.13 provider, V1.14 restore/service-manager, V1.15 hardware and memory, V1.16 observability, and V1.17 CLI/operator contract evidence lines are summarized by the closure gate and synchronized across website, docs, release notes, and i18n manifest.

progressStages.label: Signed installers, provenance, and real apply paths
progressStages.description: Future releases add provider process execution, durable stores, destructive apply gates, signed installers, OS packages, and provenance bundles.

docCards.title: User Manual
docCards.description: Install from source, validate config, run the basic loop, inspect task diagnostics, run release gates, and understand the current plan-first safety boundary.

docCards.description: How workflow Skills, runtime workers, and Lua Skills become controlled workflow capabilities.

docCards.description: Runtime-owned memory facts, knowledge retrieval, ContextBuilder policy, and Lua API boundaries.

docCards.description: How Agents, adapters, MCP servers, skills, and Lua capabilities are discovered and validated.

docCards.description: Developer tooling requirements for project indexing, Lua Agent editing, Runtime Bridge validation, dry runs, traces, and policy diagnostics.

docCards.description: Executable release readiness, security, performance, migration, and compatibility gates for the source release.

docCards.description: The V1.11.2 native archive install smoke path, upgrade and uninstall commands, release-distribution evidence, and package-manager dry-run gate.

docCards.description: The alpha prerelease notes now also summarize V1.12-V1.17 daemon, provider, restore, hardware, memory, observability, operator UX, public JSON contract, and V1.x closure evidence.

docCards.title: V1.11.4 Alpha Release Notes
docCards.description: The alpha prerelease that fixes version metadata on the CLI module split checkpoint, adds release notes, and aligns release readiness evidence with the V1.11.4 tag.

docCards.title: V1.7.4 Alpha Release Notes
docCards.description: The alpha prerelease that adds Lua shadow-load health checks, generation route gating, drain evidence, rollback audit evidence, and the Lua hot-reload lifecycle release gate.

docCards.title: V1.7.3 Alpha Release Notes
docCards.description: The alpha prerelease that adds Lua wall-clock timeout, instruction budget, cancellation token, memory budget, and the Lua resource limits release gate.

docCards.title: V1.7.2 Alpha Release Notes
docCards.description: The alpha prerelease that adds read-only Lua ctx tables, host log/audit observations, ctx.tools.call capability binding, and the Lua host bindings release gate.

docCards.title: V1.7.1 Alpha Release Notes
docCards.description: The alpha prerelease that adds the restricted Lua VM execution boundary, on_event(ctx) execution, stable Lua error mapping, and the Lua VM release gate.

docCards.title: V1.6.5 Alpha Release Notes
docCards.description: The alpha prerelease that adds durable backend diagnostics, inspect.durable JSON output, pending redrive counts, and CI/release smoke coverage.

docCards.title: V1.6.4 Alpha Release Notes
docCards.description: The alpha prerelease that adds runtime recovery scanning, ack-safe event redrive, durable recovery audit records, and the recovery release gate.

docCards.title: V1.6.3 Alpha Release Notes
docCards.description: The alpha prerelease that adds durable task snapshots, durable audit records, and artifact metadata hardening.

docCards.title: V1.6.2 Alpha Release Notes
docCards.description: The alpha prerelease that adds filesystem EventLog, DurableEventBus, queryable dead-letter storage, and durable redrive evidence.

docCards.title: V1.6.1 Alpha Release Notes
docCards.description: The alpha prerelease that starts the durable runtime/storage line with the backend manifest, layout, and migration-lock baseline.

docCards.title: V1.5.1 Release Notes
docCards.description: The patch release that keeps V1.5 contracts stable while enabling GitHub Packages delivery through GHCR.

feedback.title: Feedback and suggestions
feedback.body: Send documentation, architecture, release-gate, compatibility, package, or use-case feedback by email. Eva-CLI is at the V1.11.5-alpha CLI runtime command completion checkpoint, so concrete reports about command output, docs, package pulls, release evidence gates, backend diagnostics, generation route gates, rollback audit evidence, typed event emission, Agent lifecycle evidence, and capability routing are especially useful.
feedback.subjectPlaceholder: For example: add a quick start example
feedback.messagePlaceholder: Describe the suggestion, problem, or missing content.
feedback.note: Submitting opens your local email client and sends the message to iyetmos@gmail.com.
